title: Copenhagen Boat Rental: A Budget Guide to Sailing the Canals and Coast
html:
Exploring Copenhagen from the water is surprisingly affordable if you know where to look. Many visitors start from Nyhavn or Islands Brygge, and a three-hour self-drive motorboat can cost as little as EUR 120 for the boat, shared among up to eight people. BoatHire24 connects you with trusted owners offering exactly this kind of experience, from budget-friendly skippered charters in Copenhagen to luxury yachts.

Where to set off in Copenhagen

Copenhagen's harbour is a network of active marinas and quiet inlets. The most convenient departure points include Svanemøllehavnen in the north, close to Østerbro, and Slusen at the southern entrance to the inner harbour. Further out, the marinas at Rungsted and Vedbæk offer direct access to the Øresund. For a quick escape, the islands of Christianshavn and Refshaleøen provide sheltered water within minutes of city-centre moorings. You can skippered charters in Copenhagen for your chosen dates.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Copenhagen?

A self-drive motorboat starts around EUR 120 for three hours, while a crewed yacht might cost EUR 800 for a full day. Prices vary by season and boat size, but here is a typical breakdown: One option worth a look is the Mefasa 90 charter page.

  • Shared guided tour: from EUR 40 per person for a two-hour canal trip.
  • Small motorboat (half day, unskippered): from EUR 150 for four hours.
  • Catamaran (full day, skippered): from EUR 700 for up to ten guests.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full day): from EUR 1,200, including crew and fuel.

Which boat to rent in Copenhagen

BoatHire24 lists a wide selection of craft suitable for both beginners and experienced skippers. The Mefasa 90 is a popular choice for a private day trip. To see more options, browse the Mefasa 90 charter page for full specifications and availability.

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 guests), Ideal for a relaxed harbour cruise and short trips to Amager Strandpark; from EUR 150 for half a day.
  2. Sailboat (8, 10 guests), Perfect for exploring the Øresund coast, with the option to moor at Dragør; from EUR 400 for a full day.
  3. Catamaran (up to 12 guests), Offers stability and space for groups wanting to anchor near Middelgrunden; from EUR 700 for a full day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (10, 14 guests), For a premium trip to the ruins at Hammershus or a celebration; from EUR 1,200 for a full day.
  5. RIB or speedboat (4, 6 guests), A thrilling ride across the Øresund to Sweden; from EUR 200 for two hours.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Copenhagen?

For most boats under 15 metres with an engine below 40 kW, no licence is required, but always confirm with the operator. Danish rules exempt small motorboats up to 40 kW from any mandatory certificate. For larger vessels, an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) is standard. On BoatHire24 each listing states the licence requirement clearly. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Copenhagen or list your own. You can also see more on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Local tip: book your boat for a weekday in June to enjoy the famous Midsummer Eve bonfires from the water near Holmen, a truly Danish experience.

Season for boating in Copenhagen

The main boating season runs from May to September, with peak conditions in July and August when the prevailing westerly wind, the vestenvind, is lightest. In 2026 the summer solstice falls on a Saturday, making late June particularly popular for evening cruises. Spring and early autumn offer quieter waters and lower prices. The distance from Nyhavn to the picturesque fishing village of Dragør is about ten nautical miles, a comfortable two-hour sail.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 40 (shared tour) to EUR 1,200 (crewed yacht) per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Typical boat capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: Euro (EUR) widely accepted; check listing

Questions about boat rental in Copenhagen

Can I rent a boat in Copenhagen without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats are available for hire without any licence, provided you follow a brief safety briefing from the owner.

What is the best area for sailing in Copenhagen?

The sheltered inner harbour and the open Øresund offer varied experiences, with the islands of Amager and Refshaleøen being popular for day trips.

How many people can fit on a rented boat in Copenhagen?

Most boats accommodate between six and twelve guests, though larger yachts can take up to fourteen.

Is it cheaper to rent a boat in the morning or afternoon?

Morning slots are often slightly cheaper and the water is calmer, making them a budget-friendly choice.

Whether you are planning a family day out or a corporate event on the Øresund, the BoatHire24 marketplace offers verified boats at transparent prices. If you own a boat in the Danish capital, listing your vessel can turn empty berths into steady income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Copenhagen and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
